mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or positive 20-day returns dominate in >60% of observations when FEDFUNDS > 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an imminent labor market contraction that reduces aggregate demand and corporate profit expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample backtest.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising retail sales and declining policy rates creates a favorable earnings environment where consumer spending outpaces financing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression of 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects robust manufacturing activity that boosts short-term market sentiment and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during post-shock recovery periods with a t-statistic magnitude > 2.0.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.02 or correlation with forward returns turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds the 300 threshold, persistent inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >70% of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds 300.0.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and rise in the unemployment rate confirms a broad economic contraction that severely impacts corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with a classification accuracy above 55% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal renewed confidence in the construction sector and broader economic activity, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns during early-cycle expansion phases.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when applied to early-cycle expansion periods.
